Codepliant
PricingDocsGitHub

Free Tool

Free Privacy Policy Generator for Developers

Most privacy policy generators ask you to fill out a questionnaire. Codepliant reads your code instead. It scans your codebase for cookies, analytics, databases, auth providers, and API integrations, then generates a privacy policy that accurately reflects your application.

Code scanning vs. questionnaires

Traditional privacy policy generators rely on you knowing every data practice in your application. Do you use cookies? Which analytics provider? What personal data do you store? Miss one checkbox, and your privacy policy is inaccurate.

Codepliant takes a different approach. It performs static analysis on your source code, scanning for patterns that indicate data collection, storage, and processing. It detects ORM schemas (Prisma, Drizzle, Mongoose, TypeORM, Sequelize, Django, SQLAlchemy), authentication flows, analytics SDKs, payment integrations, and more.

The result is a privacy policy generated from evidence, not guesswork. Across 100 real-world GitHub repositories, Codepliant achieves 97.8% detection precision.

What Codepliant detects in your code

Cookies & session management
Google Analytics, Mixpanel, Segment
Prisma, Drizzle, Mongoose schemas
OAuth, JWT, SSO authentication
Stripe, PayPal payment processing
Email collection & newsletters
File uploads & storage
Geolocation & IP tracking
AI/ML model usage (OpenAI, etc.)
Third-party API integrations

Example output preview

# Privacy Policy

Last updated: March 15, 2026

## Data We Collect

Based on our analysis of this application's source code,
the following data collection practices were identified:

### Personal Information
- Email addresses (via authentication flow)
- User profiles (stored in PostgreSQL via Prisma)
- Payment information (processed by Stripe)

### Automatically Collected Data
- Cookies (session management, 3 cookies detected)
- Analytics data (Google Analytics, Mixpanel)
- IP addresses (server logs)
- Device information (user-agent parsing)

### Third-Party Services
- Stripe (payment processing)
- Google Analytics (usage analytics)
- Mixpanel (event tracking)
- AWS S3 (file storage)
- OpenAI API (AI-powered features)

## Your Rights Under GDPR
...

How to generate your privacy policy

1. Run the scanner in your project directory
npx codepliant go
2. Review the generated documents

Codepliant creates a /compliance directory with your privacy policy and other documents.

3. Customize and publish

Add your company name, contact details, and any additional clauses. The generated policy is yours to modify.

Generate your privacy policy now

Free, open source, no account required.

npx codepliant go

Frequently asked questions

How does Codepliant generate a privacy policy?

Codepliant scans your codebase for data collection patterns: cookies, analytics SDKs (Google Analytics, Mixpanel, Segment), authentication providers, ORMs (Prisma, Drizzle, Mongoose, TypeORM, Sequelize), and API integrations. It then generates a legally-informed privacy policy that accurately reflects what your application actually does.

Is this better than online privacy policy generators?

Yes. Traditional generators ask you to fill out questionnaires and hope you know every data practice in your app. Codepliant reads your source code directly, achieving 97.8% detection precision across 100 real-world GitHub repositories. It catches things you might forget to mention.

What data practices does Codepliant detect?

Codepliant detects cookies, local storage, session management, authentication (OAuth, JWT, SSO), database schemas and ORMs, analytics and tracking, payment processing, email collection, file uploads, geolocation, device fingerprinting, AI/ML model usage, third-party API calls, and more.

Is the generated privacy policy GDPR compliant?

Codepliant generates privacy policies that include GDPR-required sections: data controller information, legal basis for processing, data subject rights, data retention, international transfers, and cookie disclosures. However, we recommend having a lawyer review the final document for your specific jurisdiction.

How much does it cost?

The CLI is free and open source. You can generate privacy policies in Markdown at no cost. Pro ($29/mo) adds HTML, PDF output, and custom branding. Team ($79/mo) adds monorepo support and a compliance API.
